notes:
2560×1440 all settings max = 60fps

title: Dragon's Dogma: Dark Arisen

notes:
The game runs great, but with 2 problems:
- For no reason, the game goes from 60 FPS to 40 FPS and goes up again.
- The online services stops working shortly after entering in a save game. I do not know if this is a problem with Capcon servers or if it is a problem running the game with proton.
title: Dragon's Dogma: Dark Arisen

notes: FPS drops almost everywhere, especially during cutscenes. Generally playable, but only offline tested.
title: Dragon's Dogma: Dark Arisen

notes: On Windows, 60 fps using max settings.  With Proton, framerate variable but playable on mix of medium and high settings.  Game says "server unavailable," but I'm unsure whether that is factual or not.

title: Dragon's Dogma: Dark Arisen

notes: Game has minor drops, otherwise runs at 60fps on 1440p with everything maxed out. Had an issue where using the Alt key + LMB or RMB caused the window to lose focus. Seems to be an issue with the KDE Window Actions bound by Alt + mouse actions. Setting the modifier key here to Meta seems to have resolved the issue.

notes: Everything works straight out of the Box, great Performance, Controller works, no issues.
title: Dragon's Dogma: Dark Arisen

notes:
Had to enable CSMT in regedit for the performance to reach acceptable level.

protontricks regedit 367500 -> HKCU - Software - Wine - Direct3D (had to create it) - CSMT (created new DWORD) with a value of 1
title: Dragon's Dogma: Dark Arisen

notes: With Proton 4.2 it realy runs well out of the box. Played several hourse and had no problems so far.
title: Dragon's Dogma: Dark Arisen

notes: Outside of the opening title background video not playing, everything seems to work. The major issue is that I suffer from a big performance hit running this on Linux compared to Windows. While for the most part I'm hitting 60+, the framerate will slow at points which never happened on Windows. I'm not familiar with protontricks so I couldn't really follow along with the recommendations on the page. If anyone wants to post a more comprehensive guide to improving performance, it'd would be greatly appreciated.
title: Dragon's Dogma: Dark Arisen

notes: Game runs like garbage most of the time, some areas i get about 45 fps, most other areas are below 20 fps even with all low settings. Tried to edit the config.ini file to use all cores but it did not help.
title: Dragon's Dogma: Dark Arisen

notes:
Runs out of the box, albeit with poor performance (dipped to 30 FPS no matter what settings). However, running d9vk fixed these issues, and my average performance shot up to 120 FPS. 

There's also some graphical glitches if you alt tab, but I'm not sure if this is related to that.
title: Dragon's Dogma: Dark Arisen

notes: Runs as it should out of the box with some performance drops. HOWEVER running the game with D9VK cleared up the performance issues entirely. To enable in steam make sure you are using at least proton 4.11 and in the launch options for the game put "PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 %command%" without quotes
title: Dragon's Dogma: Dark Arisen

notes:
With Proton 4.11-1, which includes D9VK, DDDA will now play about as well on Linux as it does on Windows with the same hardware, which is a huge improvement over previous versions of Proton.  Game still gives "no server access" error when saving, however, but pawns are still available to recruit.
Use launch option:  PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 %command%
title: Dragon's Dogma: Dark Arisen

notes: runs amazing with D9VK
title: Dragon's Dogma: Dark Arisen

cpu: AMD Ryzen 3 2200G with Radeon Vega Graphics
notes: Everything works except for two FMV cutscenes, the first one plays at the end of the prologue area and the second one after defeating the dragon. After 60+ hours of playing through the game multiple times, I didn't run into any other issues. Enable D9VK for better performance.
title: Dragon's Dogma: Dark Arisen

notes: The performance is great when using d9vk as others have noted, but I also had an issue with the audio crackling.  I added PULSE_LATENCY_MSEC=60 to the launch options and now it seems to run and sound great!
title: Dragon's Dogma: Dark Arisen

notes: Game runs buttery smooth with D9VK enabled. Audio sounded fine with only issue found was with resurrecting pawns where the sound was cut short. Pawn uploading gives out an error where you cannot connect to the server, but network pawns are available and they can be hired.
title: Dragon's Dogma: Dark Arisen
